title: Greenhouse Company Handbook
content:
# Greenhouse Company Handbook

Welcome to Greenhouse. We build analytics tooling for product teams.

## Our mission

Help product teams answer their own data questions in seconds, without a data analyst in the loop.

## Values

- **Customer truth over opinions** — we ship what the data says users need.
- **Small, reversible steps** — prefer many small releases to one big bang.
- **Write it down** — decisions live in the knowledge base, not in someone's head.

## Working hours

We are async-first. Core overlap hours are 10:00–14:00 in your local timezone. See the [Remote Work Policy](/kb/remote-work-policy) for details.

## Getting help

Ask the assistant first — it can search this handbook and every published doc. If it can't answer, post in #ask-anything.
_summary: Greenhouse' mission, core values (customer truth, small reversible steps, write it down), async-first working hours, and how to get help.
_questions: What are the core overlap hours?

title: Remote Work Policy
content:
# Remote Work Policy

Greenhouse is a fully remote company.

## Where you can work

Work from anywhere within ±3 hours of the US Pacific timezone so there is enough daily overlap.

## Core hours

Be reachable **10:00–14:00** in your local time on working days. Outside that, async is the default — no expectation of instant replies.

## Home office stipend

- **$1,000** one-time setup stipend for desk, chair, and monitor.
- **$75/month** toward internet and coworking.

Submit receipts through the assistant (`/expense`) or in #ops.

## Time off

Unlimited PTO with a **15-day minimum** per year. Book more than 3 consecutive days two weeks ahead where possible.

## Equipment

Engineering gets a company laptop. Everyone else may expense up to $2,000 every 3 years.
_summary: Fully-remote policy: work within ±3h of US Pacific, core hours 10:00–14:00 local, $1,000 setup + $75/mo stipend, unlimited PTO with a 15-day minimum, and equipment rules.
_questions: What is the home office stipend?
What are the core working hours?
How much PTO do we get?
What timezones can I work from?

title: Engineering Onboarding
content:
# Engineering Onboarding

Your first week on the platform team.

## Day 1

1. Get added to GitHub, the cloud account, and PagerDuty.
2. Clone `greenhouse/platform` and run `make dev` — this boots Postgres, the API, and the web app.
3. Read the [API Style Guide](/kb/api-style-guide).

## Day 2–3

- Pick a `good-first-issue` and open a PR. Aim to ship something small by end of week.
- Pair with your onboarding buddy for one code review.

## Local setup

```bash
git clone git@github.com:greenhouse/platform.git
cd platform && make dev
```

If `make dev` fails, check Docker is running and port 5432 is free.

## On-call

You join the on-call rotation after 4 weeks. Read the [Incident Response Runbook](/kb/incident-response-runbook) before your first shift.

title: API Style Guide
content:
# API Style Guide

Conventions for the Greenhouse public and internal APIs.

## Naming

- Resources are plural nouns: `/events`, `/dashboards`.
- Use `snake_case` for JSON fields, `kebab-case` for paths.

## Versioning

All public endpoints live under `/v1`. Breaking changes require a new version; never break `/v1`.

## Errors

Return a consistent shape:

```json
{ "error": { "code": "rate_limited", "message": "Too many requests" } }
```

Use standard HTTP status codes. `4xx` = client, `5xx` = us.

## Pagination

Cursor-based only. Return `next_cursor`; never offset pagination on large tables.

## Auth

Bearer API keys in the `Authorization` header. Keys are scoped and rate-limited per client.

title: Product Roadmap 2026
content:
# Product Roadmap 2026

## Q3 (current)

- **Self-serve dashboards** — let users build dashboards without SQL. *(shipping this quarter)*
- **Slack alerts** — push metric anomalies to Slack.

## Q4

- **Cohort analysis** v1.
- **Public API GA** — promote the beta API to general availability.

## Under consideration (not committed)

- Mobile app.
- Warehouse-native deployment.

Priorities are reviewed monthly by Leadership. Ship dates are targets, not promises.
_summary: 2026 roadmap: Q3 self-serve dashboards and Slack alerts; Q4 cohort analysis and Public API GA; mobile and warehouse-native under consideration.
_questions: When does the public API go GA?

title: Incident Response Runbook
content:
# Incident Response Runbook

## Severity levels

| Sev | Meaning | Response |
|-----|---------|----------|
| SEV1 | Full outage / data loss | Page immediately, all hands |
| SEV2 | Major feature down | Page on-call, fix within 2h |
| SEV3 | Degraded / minor | Next business day |

## First 15 minutes

1. Acknowledge the page in PagerDuty.
2. Open an incident channel: `#inc-YYYYMMDD-short-name`.
3. Post the current impact and who is the incident commander (IC).
4. Mitigate first, root-cause later.

## Communication

The IC posts a status update every 30 minutes until resolved. Customer-facing updates go through the status page — coordinate with marketing.

## After

Write a blameless postmortem within 3 business days and link it here.

title: Brand Voice Guidelines
content:
# Brand Voice Guidelines

How Greenhouse sounds in docs, marketing, and support.

## Personality

Clear, confident, and helpful. We are the calm expert, not the hype machine.

## Do

- Lead with the benefit, then the feature.
- Use plain words: "see your data", not "leverage data assets".
- Short sentences. Active voice.

## Don't

- No exclamation-point spam.
- Avoid jargon: "synergy", "paradigm", "revolutionary".
- Never overpromise on the roadmap.

## Examples

> ❌ Unlock revolutionary insights with our AI-powered synergy platform!
>
> ✅ Ask a question about your product data and get an answer in seconds.

title: Customer FAQ
content:
# Customer FAQ

Common questions from customers and prospects. This doc is used by the public assistant.

## What is Greenhouse?

Greenhouse is analytics for product teams. Connect your data, ask questions in plain English, and get charts and answers without writing SQL.

## How does pricing work?

We charge per tracked monthly active user (MAU). The free tier covers up to 1,000 MAU. Paid plans start at $99/month.

## Is my data secure?

Data is encrypted in transit and at rest. We are SOC 2 Type II (in progress) and never sell customer data.

## Can I export my data?

Yes. Every dashboard and dataset can be exported to CSV, and the API lets you pull raw events.

## Do you offer a trial?

Yes — a 14-day free trial of any paid plan, no credit card required.
_summary: Public-facing FAQ: what Greenhouse is, MAU-based pricing (free up to 1,000 MAU, paid from $99/mo), security posture, data export options, and a 14-day trial.
_questions: Do you offer a free trial?

title: Q3 2026 OKRs (Draft)
content:
# Q3 2026 OKRs — DRAFT

> Status: draft, pending Leadership sign-off.

## Objective 1: Make dashboards self-serve

- KR1: 60% of new dashboards created without SQL.
- KR2: Median time-to-first-dashboard < 10 minutes.

## Objective 2: Grow paid conversion

- KR1: Trial → paid conversion from 12% to 18%.
- KR2: 20 new paid teams.

## Objective 3: Ship reliably

- KR1: 99.9% API uptime.
- KR2: Zero SEV1 incidents.

title: Security Architecture Notes
content:
# Security Architecture Notes

Working notes on our security model. Shared with Engineering while it firms up.

## Data isolation

Each customer's events live in a row-level-secured table keyed by `org_id`. Every query is scoped by a mandatory `org_id` predicate enforced in the data layer.

## Secrets

All secrets are AES-256-GCM encrypted at rest with a key from the environment. No plaintext credentials in the database.

## API keys

Stored only as SHA-256 hashes. The raw key is shown once at creation and never again.

## Open questions

- Do we need customer-managed encryption keys for the enterprise tier?
- Audit log retention window — 90 days or 1 year?
